about.title: Karen Zhu
about.subtitle: About Me
aboutParagraphs: I'm a business student with a strong passion for design. Growing up taking art classes, I always enjoyed expressing my creativity in visual forms and found that I had a talent for it.

I decided to pursue business as a degree because of the flexibility of being able to mould my interests with work and school. Like art, business requires thoughtful solutions that are both creative and practical. In my first year of classes at the University of Toronto, I particularly enjoyed my marketing classes because they heavily involved a creative components and offer opportunities to expand into design.

To further explore this area of interest, in July of 2024, I completed the Google UX Design course and learned how to develop wireframes and mockups on Figma. Furthermore, I placed in the final of the 2024 Design Dash competition hosted by Rotman Commerce by applying these skills. This experience revealed a strong interest to continue pursuing creative opportunities as I not only enjoyed them, but had a talent for it.
I strive to use design and business strategy to create innovative and accessible user experiences. Please reach out to learn a bit more about me!
funFacts: I enjoy making different cafe style drinks! My favourites: matcha lattes, lavender london fogs, Hong Kong style milk tea, yuzu tea. I like visiting local cafes and rating their drinks for inspiration.
I have a passion for fashion - it's another creative form of expression and I believe in dressing for success. I also admire creative marketing in the fashion industry.

items.title: Rotman Commerce Fashion Group

cards.title: About
bodyLines: A fashion business club run by University of Toronto students that revolves around core values of education, community, and networking in the fashion industry.
The purpose of RCFG is to provide Rotman Commerce students the opportunity to connect on fashion and beauty topics such as global trends, styling advice, fashion careers, and business fashion.
We host engaging events and create community within the world of fashion.
Join us to expand your network and passion for the industry.

cards.title: My Journey
bodyLines: Director of Creative Content Feb 2024 - Present
Manager of Creative Content Feb 2023 - Feb 2024

items.artworkBody: The Creative Content team was responsible for designing artwork for this year's team. Below are some rough ideas and iterations upon receiving feedback:
items.creativePostsIntro: Our team thinks of creative ways to promote club events and, when there are no upcoming events, executes large creative projects such as street interviews and Instagram takeovers to keep our audience entertained and maintain consistent uploads.
items.thriftStats:
This September, to promote our Thrifting in Toronto event, we created a scrapbook style post to showcase executive team thrift finds.
759 Accounts Reached · 85.7% from followers · 14.3% from non-followers.
items.creativeReelsBody: Event Recaps, Day in the Life, and more coming soon...
